candidates.why: Separate same-level operating D&A summary exists above operating profit, so some D&A is disclosed outside the selected COGS component rows. However, the row is a company-wide operating total including amortization and impairment and is not specifically labelled as COGS/cost of sales, so assigning it to COGS would risk misallocation and double counting.
Separate operating depreciation row exists, but it is not labelled as COGS-specific and appears to be part of the broader D&A disclosure rather than a cost-of-sales-only amount.

Separate depreciation component exists above operating profit, but it is not labelled as SG&A-specific and appears to be part of a broader operating D&A block.
Could plausibly relate to overhead/SG&A, but the statement does not indicate functional assignment to SG&A versus other buckets.

However, the excerpt does not provide SG&A-specific D&A rows. Because the visible rows are company-wide operating D&A/components rather than SG&A-labelled amounts, conservative treatment is to add nothing automatically.
